commitMessage: Add T key to pause/resume board auto-rotation (#19)

topics.title: Add keyboard shortcut to pause and resume board auto-rotation
topics.trigger: Players wanted a way to temporarily freeze the board's automatic spinning without having to manually rotate it themselves or lose their current viewing angle.
topics.response:
- Added a boolean toggle flag to the game class to track whether auto-rotation is user-paused, and wired it into the rotation update loop in `src/main.ts` so that rotation stops advancing when the flag is set.
- Added a `toggleAutoRotatePause()` method that flips the pause flag when auto-rotation is active, bound to the T key in the keyboard event handler.
- Updated the on-screen controls text for both PvP and bot-vs-bot modes to include the new T key hint.
topics.decisions: The pause toggle was implemented as a separate boolean flag rather than reusing the existing timed-override mechanism (which temporarily suppresses rotation after a manual Q/E rotation). Keeping them separate means the T key pause persists indefinitely until the player presses T again, while the timed override continues to work independently for its own purpose. Merging the two would have required tracking intent and complicated the expiry logic.

recap: Players can now press T during a game to freeze the board's automatic spinning in place, then press T again to resume it. The pause holds indefinitely until toggled off, giving players full control over their viewing angle without interrupting gameplay. Both the two-player and bot-vs-bot modes display the new shortcut in the on-screen controls bar so players can discover it without consulting documentation.
